preamble: The phonetic transformation from a voiced alveolar lateral fricative (ɬ) to a voiceless glottal fricative (h) involves a significant reduction in articulatory stricture and a shift in the place of articulation. This change typically results in the loss of laterality and the devoicing of the sound, moving from a complex sibilant to a simpler, breathier glottal sound.
phoneticEffects: Devoicing, loss of laterality, shift in place of articulation from alveolar to glottal, reduction of articulatory stricture
sources: Reconstructive Description of Eighteenth-century Xinka Grammar Volume I - LOT Publications.
